briefs.headline: Tadej Pogacar wins Paris-Nice, extends dominance over Tour de France rival Wout van Aert
briefs.lede: Tadej Pogacar claimed a third consecutive win at Paris-Nice on Sunday, beating his close rival Wout van Aert in the final stage to underline his dominance ahead of this summer's Tour de France.

stories.title: Tadej Pogacar wins Paris-Nice
stories.body: Pogacar took the lead after stage 7 and never looked back, with Van Aert finishing second overall, 1:30 behind. The Slovenian has now won four races in a row, including three consecutive victories at UAE Tour, Tirreno-Adriatico, and Paris-Nice.

stories.title: Pogacar extends dominance over Van Aert
stories.body: With both riders expected to be key contenders at the Tour de France this summer, Sunday's win by Pogacar will provide a significant psychological boost. "It was a tough race, but I'm happy with how things turned out," said Pogacar after the finish.

stories.title: New Specialized S-Works Venge disc gains Paris-Nice approval
stories.body: Specialized's new S-Works Venge disc bike made its debut at Paris-Nice this week, with a number of top riders using it in competition. The aerodynamic time trial bike features disc brakes and is expected to become popular among professional cyclists as the technology becomes more widespread.

stories.title: Paris-Nice fan encourages riders with personal message
stories.body: A fan along the route at Paris-Nice held up a sign reading "Go Tadej, keep the dream alive" during stage 8. The rider's mother had passed away recently, and Pogacar dedicated his win to her. Fans have long played an important role in cycling, providing support and motivation during races.

quote.text: I dedicate this win to my mom.

briefs.headline: UAE Tour stage 4: Dutch rider Tiesj Benoot wins as Tadej Pogacar takes overall lead
briefs.lede: Tiesj Benoot of Israel-Premier Tech stormed to victory on stage 4 of the UAE Tour, while Slovenian rider Tadej Pogacar took over the overall lead.

stories.title: UAE Tour Stage 4: Benoot Claims Victory as Pogacar Takes Overall Lead
stories.body: The 207km stage from Al Ain to Jebel Hafeet was a grueling test for riders. Israel-Premier Tech's Tiesj Benoot attacked on the final climb to take his first win of the season. Pogacar's teammate Brandon McNulty placed second.

stories.title: Wout van Aert to Skip Volta ao Algarve
stories.body: The Belgian rider will skip the Portuguese event due to fatigue after a busy start to the season, affecting his preparation for Milano-Sanremo.

stories.title: Specialized Launches New S-Works Venge SBC
stories.body: The American manufacturer released its latest aero road bike featuring an advanced cockpit optimised for airflow, already used by Sagan and Evenepoel.

stories.title: Strava Launches Virtual Club Challenge Series
stories.body: Strava announced a new series of virtual club challenges including hill climbs, time trials and group rides with prizes for winning clubs.

quote.text: The final climb was brutal, but I knew I had a chance to win.
